audienceResearch: Public-page evidence is collected from the live website through browser sessions. Raw HTML returned little text, so this cohort is explicitly directional and based on the product name/domain, likely learning offer, and visible-session evidence gathered by bots.
researchSources: public: https://self-degree.com/ live browser surface on 2026-07-04
assumption: product is education/self-directed learning based on name and URL
assumption: visitors include learners, career switchers, founders, and skeptical buyers

audienceSegments.name: Career switcher
audienceSegments.evidence: Assumption from education/self-degree category; needs credible path and outcome proof.
jobs: understand what skill path is offered

audienceSegments.name: Self-directed learner
audienceSegments.evidence: Assumption from product name; self-learners compare structure and momentum.
jobs: check whether it fits current level

audienceSegments.name: Parent or sponsor
audienceSegments.evidence: Education products often have buyers who are not the learner; this is marked as assumption.
jobs: decide if it is worth paying for

audienceSegments.name: Busy founder learning a practical skill
audienceSegments.evidence: Assumption: founders use self-guided learning when speed matters.

roles.role: career switcher deciding whether this can replace another course
roles.past: I have bought online courses before and often quit when the path became vague after the first week.
roles.discovery: I found Self Degree while searching for a more independent way to learn.
roles.goal: Understand what I would learn, how long it takes, and whether there is proof it works.
roles.lifeGoal: Build a career that gives me independence without wasting money on empty promises.

roles.past: I like teaching myself, but I need structure and examples or I drift.
roles.discovery: Someone shared the site as an alternative to traditional degrees.
roles.goal: Find a sample curriculum, concrete outcome, or first lesson.
roles.lifeGoal: Become genuinely capable, not just collect another motivational bookmark.

roles.role: parent evaluating whether the offer is serious
roles.past: I have seen many learning products overpromise, so I look for safety, support, and realistic outcomes.
roles.discovery: My teenager sent me the link and asked what I thought.
roles.goal: Decide whether the product feels credible enough to discuss further.
roles.lifeGoal: Help my family make education decisions that create real options.

roles.role: busy founder looking for practical upskilling
roles.past: I need fast practical learning and will leave if the offer sounds abstract.
roles.discovery: I opened the link between meetings after seeing it mentioned online.
roles.goal: Check whether there is a clear first step and practical result.
roles.lifeGoal: Spend scarce attention only on learning that compounds into better decisions.

ideaRules.idea: Show a concrete sample learning path before asking for commitment.
Explain price, time, and expected outcome near the first CTA.
Make outcomes specific enough for skeptical learners and sponsors.
